question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted stream file with a consensusStart value equal to the previous file's consensusEnd, exploiting the `previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()` boundary condition in `shouldParse()`, causing the file to be silently skipped and its transactions permanently omitted from the mirror node's history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `getLast()` returning null path (when `last` AtomicReference is empty and `streamFileRepository.findLatest()` returns empty) by racing a restart or cache-clear event, causing `shouldParse()` to skip hash chain verification entirely and accept any arbitrary stream file as the first in the chain, allowing history to be reorganized from an attacker-controlled anchor?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a batch list where an early file in the list has a consensusEnd that overlaps with a later file's consensusStart, causing `shouldParse()` to silently skip the later file via the `continue` branch, resulting in transactions from that file being permanently absent from the parsed history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch list where all files are skipped by `shouldParse()` (e.g., all have consensusStart <= previous consensusEnd), causing `initial == previous` to remain true and `doFlush()` to never be called, leaving the `last` AtomicReference stale and causing subsequent legitimate files to be rejected due to incorrect hash chain state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hash chain verification is only performed when `previous.getType().isChained()` returns true, by injecting a stream file of a non-chained type, bypassing the `expectedHash.contentEquals(actualHash)` check entirely and inserting an arbitrary file into the parsed history without a valid hash link?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `HashMismatchException` to be thrown repeatedly by injecting a file with a mismatched `previousHash`, triggering the `@Retryable` mechanism in `RecordFileParser.parse()` indefinitely, creating a denial-of-service that halts all subsequent transaction ingestion and effectively freezes the mirror node's transaction history at a chosen point?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a v6 stream file with a manipulated `index` field that is far ahead of the last v5 file's index, causing `updateIndex()` to compute a large positive `offset` and call `repository.updateIndex(offset)`, which bulk-shifts all existing block numbers in the database and reorganizes the entire historical block index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a v6 stream file with an `index` value smaller than the last v5 file's index plus one, causing `updateIndex()` to compute a negative `offset` and call `repository.updateIndex(offset)` with a negative value, shifting all historical block numbers downward and corrupting the block index used by downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a stream file whose `dateRangeFilter` excludes all contained `RecordItem` consensus timestamps, causing `recordItemListener.onItem()` to never be called for any transaction in the file, while `streamFileListener.onEnd()` is still called, resulting in the file being recorded as processed but all its transactions silently dropped from history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch `parse(List<T>)` loop where `doFlush()` is only called once on the last successfully parsed file, by crafting a list where only the first file passes `shouldParse()` but the last file does not, causing `doFlush()` to be called on an intermediate file and the `last` AtomicReference to be set to a non-final state, corrupting the hash chain anchor for future files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the in-memory `last` AtomicReference being null and `streamFileRepository.findLatest()` returning a stale or manipulated record, causing `shouldParse()` to use an incorrect previous file for hash chain validation and accept a forged stream file as the legitimate next file in the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file with a `consensusEnd` value set to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing the `parseLatencyMetric.record(Duration.between(consensusInstant, Instant.now()))` call to produce a negative duration or overflow, and also causing all subsequent files to be skipped by `shouldParse()` because their `consensusStart` will always be less than `Long.MAX_VALUE`, permanently halting transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a list containing a single stream file that passes `shouldParse()` but whose `doParse()` throws an exception, causing the `catch` block to set `success = false` and rethrow, while `doFlush()` is never called, leaving the `last` AtomicReference unchanged and allowing the attacker to repeatedly attempt injection of the same file position without advancing the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doFlush()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `doFlush()` calls `streamFile.clear()` after `last.set(streamFile)`, meaning the `last` AtomicReference holds a reference to a cleared (bytes=null, items=empty) stream file, and if `getLast()` returns this cleared file, the hash value used for chain verification may be null or empty, allowing a subsequent file with any `previousHash` to pass the `contentEquals` check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file where `getHash()` returns null (the default implementation in the `StreamFile` interface), causing `expectedHash.contentEquals(actualHash)` in `shouldParse()` to throw a NullPointerException that bypasses the `HashMismatchException` path and instead triggers the generic exception handler, potentially allowing retry-based exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch processing path where `count` accumulates `streamFile.getCount()` from each parsed file, by injecting a file with a manipulated `count` field that does not reflect the actual number of transactions processed, causing the logged batch summary to misrepresent the number of ingested transactions and masking a history gap?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `getProperties()` method returning a mutable `ParserProperties` reference, and if any downstream component modifies the returned properties (e.g., calling `setEnabled(false)`), cause the parser to stop processing all subsequent stream files, effectively freezing the transaction history at the current state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file with a `getPreviousHash()` returning an empty string, exploiting the `SHA_384.isHashEmpty()` check in the downloader's `verifyHashChain()` that returns true for empty hashes, causing the hash chain verification to be skipped with only a warning logged, and allowing the file to be accepted without a valid chain link?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a batch where the first file has `previous == null` (no prior state), causing `shouldParse()` to return true unconditionally for the first file without any hash verification, and then chain subsequent forged files off this unverified anchor, reorganizing history from the very first ingested file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `RecordFile` containing `RecordItem` objects with consensus timestamps that fall within the `dateRangeFilter` window but are out of chronological order relative to each other, causing `recordItemListener.onItem()` to process transactions in a non-sequential order and store them with incorrect relative ordering in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Transactional` annotation on `RecordFileParser.parse()` by causing a transaction timeout (via the configurable `transactionTimeout` property) mid-parse, resulting in a partial rollback where some `RecordItem` objects were processed by `recordItemListener.onItem()` but the `streamFileListener.onEnd()` was not called, leaving the database in an inconsistent state with orphaned transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that in the batch `parse(List<T>)` path, `doFlush()` is only called on the last successfully parsed file (`previous`), meaning if an exception occurs mid-batch after several files have been `doParse()`d but before `doFlush()`, those files' transactions are rolled back but the `last` AtomicReference is not updated, causing the next batch attempt to re-process already-attempted files and potentially double-insert transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the v5-to-v6 migration path in `updateIndex()` by supplying a stream file with `version >= 6` when the last file has `version < 6`, and set the new file's `index` to an attacker-controlled value, causing `repository.updateIndex(offset)` to shift all historical block numbers by an arbitrary offset and reorganize the entire block index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `synchronized` keyword on `RecordFileParser.parse()` being per-instance, and if multiple `RecordFileParser` instances exist (e.g., due to misconfiguration), cause concurrent parsing of overlapping stream files that both pass `shouldParse()` independently, resulting in duplicate or interleaved transaction records in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch list where a file at position N has a `consensusStart` that is exactly one nanosecond after the previous file's `consensusEnd`, passing the `shouldParse()` check, but whose `previousHash` is set to the hash of a file that is not the immediately preceding file in the canonical chain, causing a hash chain fork to be silently accepted?
